ranked_movies.title: Hereditary
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently cited as a modern masterpiece and a future classic. Praised for its intense performances, particularly Toni Collette's, and its deeply unsettling, psychological horror.

ranked_movies.title: The Thing
ranked_movies.reason: Consistently mentioned as a top-tier horror film. Celebrated for its unparalleled practical effects, body horror, and atmosphere of paranoia.

ranked_movies.title: Alien
ranked_movies.reason: Widely regarded as one of the greatest sci-fi horror films ever made. Noted for its iconic monster design, suspenseful direction, and strong protagonist.

ranked_movies.title: The Witch
ranked_movies.reason: Often listed as a modern classic. Applauded for its historical authenticity, slow-burn tension, and atmospheric dread.

ranked_movies.title: The Ring
ranked_movies.reason: Highly praised as one of the best American remakes. Noted for its dreary, wet aesthetic, overwhelming sense of doom, and cultural impact.

ranked_movies.title: It Follows
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently recommended as a unique and effective horror film. Loved for its original premise, constant sense of dread, and strong rewatch value.

ranked_movies.title: The Shining
ranked_movies.reason: A perennial favorite and undisputed classic. Recognized for its iconic performances, masterful direction, and psychological depth.

ranked_movies.title: Get Out
ranked_movies.reason: Often cited as a brilliant and socially relevant horror film. Praised for its clever writing, sharp social commentary, and strong lead performance.

ranked_movies.title: The Exorcist
ranked_movies.reason: Consistently named as one of the greatest horror films of all time. Noted for its terrifying scenes, powerful themes, and cultural significance.

ranked_movies.title: Midsommar
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently discussed for its disturbing and visually striking portrayal of grief and cults. Often appears on lists of favorite and most impactful horror films.

ranked_movies.title: Scream
ranked_movies.reason: A cornerstone of the slasher genre. Praised for its meta-commentary, witty script, and iconic characters, with many listing it as a top franchise.

ranked_movies.title: Terrifier
ranked_movies.reason: Heavily discussed for its extreme gore and graphic violence. Noted for becoming a modern cult classic with significant Halloween costume influence.

ranked_movies.title: The Substance
ranked_movies.reason: A recent film repeatedly mentioned as a potential future classic. Applauded for its body horror, strong performances, and shocking final act.

ranked_movies.title: Smile 2
ranked_movies.reason: Highly recommended for its intense, panic-inducing experience in theaters. Described as one of the most effective and overlooked horror films recently.

ranked_movies.title: Silence of the Lambs
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently listed as a must-see horror film. Celebrated for its Oscar-winning performances, psychological depth, and thriller elements.

ranked_movies.title: Hellraiser
ranked_movies.reason: Often mentioned as a creative and creepy classic. Recognized for its unique villain (Pinhead) and its exploration of pleasure and pain.

ranked_movies.title: REC
ranked_movies.reason: Highly recommended, especially for found-footage fans. Praised for its claustrophobic setting, relentless pace, and iconic stairwell scene.

ranked_movies.title: Cabin in the Woods
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently cited as a fun, meta horror-comedy. Loved for its deconstruction of horror tropes and entertaining premise.

ranked_movies.title: Event Horizon
ranked_movies.reason: Often recommended as a cult classic sci-fi horror. Noted for its brutal recovered footage scene and its connection to Warhammer 40k lore.

ranked_movies.title: Carrie
ranked_movies.reason: Consistently brought up as a film where the villain has a valid grievance. Recognized for its tragic story and impactful climax.
